ksa_text: Ability to diagnose root causes and resolve complex technical issues across system layers, conducting incident analysis and problem remediation.

Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Edge-domain Technical Foundations KSA with insufficient signal strength (composite score 0.446) to justify inclusion for the VP AI/ML. Without stronger framework alignment, this ability KSA does not meet the inclusion threshold.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No internal inconsistency identified. Domain scope, seniority fit, and signal level are internally consistent with exclusion.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Could this edge-domain ability KSA be expected in real-world VP AI/ML roles, even if not core to the role definition?
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: Recent job postings for VP AI/ML roles increasingly include Technical Foundations competencies as organizations broaden role scope. External evidence supports inclusion.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Overturn: evolving market expectations for VP AI/ML now include Technical Foundations competencies. Revised to include.

decision_rationale: Initially excluded but overturned through adversarial review. Technical Foundations ability KSA is relevant to the VP AI/ML based on external evidence and internal consistency re-evaluation.
